steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The hypothesis says warmer months produce larger-bodied flies (measured by wing size). The blank needs data that directly shows warmer month = larger male wing size.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — compares temperatures only: Never mentions wing size, so it cannot support a temperature-size link. ✗
  • Choice B — July male wing size 2.02 mm vs. October 1.98 mm: July is warmest (low 62°F) and has the larger male wing size; October is coolest (low 44°F) and has the smaller size. Directly confirms warmer = larger. ✓
  • Choice C — July female wing size stated as 2.02 mm: The table shows July female wing size as 2.31 mm, so this misreads the data. ✗
  • Choice D — consistent sex difference across months: Shows females are always larger than males but ignores the temperature-size relationship entirely. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
